zh.lifeSummary: 當Jourdain de Say Seigneur d’Aunay-sur-Odon於大約1085年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親William de Saye22歲，母親Agnes Grandmesnil17歲。他於1105年與Lucy De Rumilly在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1161年在法國奧維普-德-o賽享年77歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jourdain de Say Seigneur d’Aunay-sur-Odon于大约1085年出生在法国諾門迪时，他的父亲William de Saye22岁，母亲Agnes Grandmesnil17岁。他于1105年与Lucy De Rumilly在法国諾門迪结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1161年在法国奧維普-德-o賽享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Say
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

nameMeanings.name: Jourdain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Jourdain de Say Seigneur d’Aunay-sur-Odon was born about 1085, in Normandy, France, his father, William de Saye, was 22 and his mother, Agnes Grandmesnil, was 17. He married Lucy De Rumilly in 1105, in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1161, in Say, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jourdain de Say Seigneur d’Aunay-sur-Odon est né environ 1085, à Normandie, France, son père, William de Saye, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Agnes Grandmesnil, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Lucy De Rumilly en 1105, à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1161, à Say, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France, à l’âge de 77 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Jourdain de Say Seigneur d’Aunay-sur-Odonが約1085年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた時，父William de Sayeは22歳，母Agnes Grandmesnilは17歳であった。1105年にフランスノーマンディでLucy De Rumillyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1161年にフランスアウバーグンプウイ-デ-ドoメサイで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jourdain de Say Seigneur d’Aunay-sur-Odon(이)가 대략 1085년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Saye(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Grandmesnil(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1105년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Lucy De Rumilly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1161년 프랑스 오버그니 푸이-데-드o 지에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jourdain de Say Seigneur d’Aunay-sur-Odon родился около 1085 г. в местности норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени William de Saye было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Grandmesnil, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy De Rumilly в 1105 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1161 г. в местности сеи, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргн, Франция в возрасте 77 лет.
